reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct — publish_column_sidecars() at publish_blocks.rs:522-529 does publish all data columns to all computed subnets (derived from column_index % 128) without checking network_globals.sampling_subnets. However, this behavior is by design for block producers. When a validator produces a block, it receives blobs from its local EL via Engine API (authenticated JWT, semi-trusted per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json). The validator must publish ALL columns to enable network-wide data availability sampling. The subnet filtering to sampling_subnets occurs on the import side (publish_blocks.rs:240-244), not the publish side. The attack path requires a block producer to publish a block, and the blobs come from the co-located EL, which is a SEMI_TRUSTED source. This is the intended custody model: producers publish all columns to seed the network; non-producers subscribe only to their sampling subnets. Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 914-930, PROP-6a4369e9-inv-036: 'Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling).' The spec requirement applies to re-gossiping received columns, not to initial publishing by block producers who must distribute all columns for DA.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling). Assertion: forall col: published(col, subnet) => subscribed(subnet, custody) || subscribed(subnet, sampling)
